items.rationale: The house's foundational thesis: trustless decentralized protocols replace intermediaries, and the dominant 2023 fight is preserving that property against regulators who would force compliance onto protocols themselves.
items.age_note: Held consistently since 2021; sharpened into the 'regulate apps not protocols' frame through 2022 and restated repeatedly in Q1 2023

items.rationale: Seen as the next foundational unlock on the scale of smart contracts — enabling private DeFi, identity, scaling and broader adoption; backed with the Aztec investment.
items.age_note: Rising conviction since 2021 (zkSync/Aleo); peaked late 2022 with Aztec (Dec 2022)

items.rationale: Ethereum is treated as the clear leader by developer mindshare and security; the Merge and rollup diversity (ZK + optimistic) are celebrated as the path to scaled, secure decentralized computation.
items.age_note: Consistent; The Merge (Sept 2022) marked as a milestone

items.rationale: Positioned post-FTX as the trustless, transparent answer to CeFi failures; ongoing primitive innovation (peer-to-pool + peer-to-peer matching) seen as expanding the design space.
items.age_note: Bullish throughout; conviction reinforced (not reversed) by 2022 CeFi collapses

items.rationale: Genuine digital ownership, lower take-rates vs web2, composable IP and standardized licensing ('Can't Be Evil'); excitement matured from speculative froth toward durable collectible/community models.
items.age_note: Peak hype 2021–early 2022; cooled and matured by late 2022 ('Pokemon cards, not ultra-scarcity'), still core

items.rationale: Token incentives bootstrap real-world supply-side networks (telecom, storage) that can beat incumbents; crystallized into a DePIN unit-economics test by early 2023.
items.age_note: Web3 'token incentive' playbook from 2021 (Helium/Arweave) reframed as DePIN by Jan 2023

items.rationale: User-owned in-game assets flip the player-game relationship; on-chain logic and established studios (EVE/CCP) seen as the path to player agency and autonomy.
items.age_note: Enthusiasm since 2021 (Axie); most recent investment CCP/EVE (Mar 2023)

items.rationale: Composable, user-owned, portable social graphs on credibly neutral protocols are framed as obvious in retrospect; builders won't tolerate getting 'rugged' by centralized platforms.
items.age_note: Articulated 2022; 'obvious in retrospect' (May 2022), Farcaster investment July 2022

items.rationale: Novel internet-native coordination superior to legacy legal structures, but expectations explicitly dialed down — most DAOs are slow 'city governments,' and legal/governance plumbing is still missing.
items.age_note: High enthusiasm 2021–early 2022; visibly tempered by mid-2022 ('city governments, not dynamic startups')

items.rationale: A new and rapidly growing region of the idea maze: crypto gives AI ownership, payments and incentive alignment; speculative framings of autonomous, self-funding programs.
items.age_note: Nascent theme appearing late 2022 and growing through Q1 2023; exploratory, not yet thesis-grade

items.rationale: Moving past 'not your keys, not your coins' via MPC/multi-sig/policy wallets is seen as a key UX and self-custody unlock.
items.age_note: Stated mid-2022 (Entropy seed); not prominently restated in Q1 2023

items.rationale: The single largest drag the house perceives: no constructive guidance since April 2019, enforcement that doesn't scale, and deliberate uncertainty that benefits bad actors and drives builders offshore.
items.age_note: Building since 2021; became the dominant, most-repeated theme across late 2022 and Q1 2023

items.rationale: Centralized, opaque, often-offshore intermediaries (FTX, Celsius, Voyager, BlockFi) — not the technology — caused 2022's damage; the cycle attracts profiteers who will return.
items.age_note: Fraud flagged as the top user risk since early 2022; intensified after the FTX collapse (Nov 2022)

items.rationale: Bad collateral, not algorithms per se, blew up Terra; uncollateralized designs are dismissed outright while well-collateralized decentralized stablecoins are defended.
items.age_note: Crystallized at the UST collapse (May 2022) and carried through 2022–2023 policy advocacy

items.rationale: Deployed proof systems run at ~80 bits of security have feasible known attacks; prioritizing performance over security threatens hundreds of millions in rollup assets.
items.age_note: Raised Sept 2022; escalated to a specific systemic warning in Mar 2023

items.rationale: Sanctioning or regulating protocols (not apps) is impractical and would fragment the open internet along geopolitical lines; network-layer chokepoints could censor whole chains.
items.age_note: Sharpened after Tornado Cash sanctions (Aug 2022); restated through Q1 2023

items.rationale: Smart-contract development is costly, non-agile and hard to secure; expect more exploits until verification/audit tooling matures.
items.age_note: Long-standing; restated plainly in Mar 2023

items.rationale: Entityless DAOs expose members/multisig holders to unlimited liability and tortious-interference suits; direct-democracy token voting suffers voter apathy and capture by small groups.
items.age_note: Liability work since 2021; governance-failure critique sharpened Sept 2022

items.rationale: Spawning many chains/bridges splits crypto-economic security, making each easier to attack — 51% attacks and DeFi-collateral entanglement are warning signs.
items.age_note: Stated Mar 2023; counterweight to the team's own pro-many-chains 2021 stance

items.rationale: Centralized app stores cripple or block native crypto experiences and extract 30% take rates, throttling mainstream UX.
items.age_note: Stated Dec 2022; recurring web2-take-rate critique

items.rationale: Treated as the clear category leader by developer mindshare, fees, security and economic activity; post-Merge PoS gives it nation-state-scale economic security.
items.age_note: Consistent leader call throughout the corpus

items.rationale: Held up as a genuinely decentralized, trustless, self-sustaining protocol; the team is an active governance participant and treats it as a model DeFi primitive.
items.age_note: Consistent reference protocol 2022–2023

items.rationale: Framed as technically a better, cryptographically-scarce version of gold; long track record, deep community and security give faith in its durability.
items.age_note: Store-of-value framing appears in early-2023 talks; less central to the house than ETH

items.rationale: Defended as robust through 2022 volatility precisely because they use exogenous over-collateralization, unlike Terra; viewed as capital-efficiency unlocks for the ecosystem.
items.age_note: Defended specifically vs Terra post-May 2022

items.rationale: Extremely under-collateralized, endogenous-collateral design that collapsed; held up as the canonical example of bad-collateral failure.
items.age_note: Collapsed May 2022; cited as cautionary case through 2022–2023

items.rationale: Widely viewed with skepticism in the legal community; its public risk profile and the SEC's inaction are cited as a regulation-by-enforcement failure.
items.age_note: Collapsed Nov 2022

items.rationale: Subject of the SEC's push to deem a token a security generally; the team is pessimistic on Ripple's outcome and uses it to argue Howey applied to secondaries is bad law.
items.age_note: Ongoing litigation as of early 2023

items.rationale: No fundamental economic basis; a meme without a reason to thrive — emblematic of speculative tokens the house disdains.
items.age_note: Stated once in Nov 2021 and not restated; an old view cited for posture

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish and still deploying through the bear market — Crypto Fund 4 (May 2022), Aztec (Dec 2022) and CCP/EVE (Mar 2023) — while advising founders to keep building. But the posture is tactically defensive: the house is in a regulatory-advocacy crouch, treats US regulation-by-enforcement and post-FTX fraud fallout as severe near-term headwinds, and frames the bear market as a healthy filter that prolongs good-faith innovation rather than a reason to retreat. Net: high structural conviction, cautious near-term execution.

notes: Recency-weighted to Q1 2023. Where views shifted, the current stance is reported: ICO/utility-token enthusiasm (2017-style) is treated as dead and bad ('the era of the wild ICO is over'); DAO expectations were explicitly revised downward through 2022; NFT excitement matured from ultra-scarcity hype toward durable collectible models. Heaviest signal by volume in the recent window is anti-SEC regulatory advocacy, making 'preserve decentralization / regulate apps not protocols' both the top excitement and the mirror of the top concern. Caveat on provenance: many dated (transcript) items are guest lectures by academics and portfolio founders (e.g., EigenLayer, Compound, Chia, ZK researchers), not necessarily a16z partners — I weighted partner tweets and firm announcements higher for 'consensus' and used transcripts mainly to corroborate technical concerns (PoS long-range attacks, SNARK security, AMM loss-versus-rebalancing, smart-contract insecurity). The firm deliberately avoids shilling specific tokens, so tokens_excited reflects protocols they hold up as models (ETH, UNI) and asset-class endorsements (decentralized collateralized stablecoins, BTC-as-better-gold) rather than price calls; conviction values are intensity, not probability.
